findings.message: 'Update' is declared in allowed-tools but is not a recognized Claude Code tool; likely a leftover from an early template draft
Command takes required argument $1 (feature-name) but provides no guard or error message when $1 is empty or missing
Vague quantifiers throughout 460-line body: 'comprehensive', 'thorough', 'appropriate', 'relevant', 'key' appear 10+ times; penalty capped at -20

'Update' tool appears in allowed-tools of spec-design.md, spec-requirements.md, and spec-status.md but not in the other 7 commands; selective presence suggests an unfinished or inconsistent edit rather than intentional specialization
Command takes required argument $1 (feature-name) but provides no guard when $1 is empty
Command uses $ARGUMENTS (project description) but does not guard against an empty argument string
Mild vague language: 'clear next step' (line 80), 'brief description' (line 82) (~2 instances)
'Update' is declared in allowed-tools but is not a recognized Claude Code tool
Command takes required $1 but no guard for empty or missing argument
'comprehensive' in description, 'appropriate subject' (line 35), 'concise' (line 36) — ~3 vague instances

!bash -c 'ls -la .kiro/specs/$1/' embeds the user-supplied slash-command argument directly into a bash -c string without sanitization; a crafted feature name containing shell metacharacters executes arbitrary commands

Three commands (spec-requirements.md:21, spec-status.md:13, validate-gap.md:21) share the identical unsanitized !bash -c '$1' pattern; fix must be applied uniformly across all three to avoid partial remediation
'Update' is declared in allowed-tools but is not a recognized Claude Code tool
Command takes required $1 but no guard for empty argument; bash inline commands on lines 13-22 would silently fail or expose unexpected directory listings
'comprehensive status report' (line 24), 'appropriate' (line 73), 'clear' (line 79) — ~4 vague instances
!bash -c 'ls -la .kiro/specs/$1/ 2>/dev/null || echo ...' embeds user argument in bash -c without sanitization
Command takes required $1 but no guard for empty argument
'comprehensive context' (line 30), 'appropriate' (line 72), 'relevant' (lines 76, 108) — ~4 vague instances
'appropriate' (line 82), 'clear' (line 98), 'unique value' (line 150), 'relevant' (line 102) — ~4 vague instances
'comprehensive' x4, 'significant' x4, 'relevant' x2, 'major' x3 — 13+ vague instances, penalty capped at -20
Command takes required $1 but no guard for empty argument
'acceptable' (line 116), 'manageable' (line 116), 'significant' (line 115), 'thorough' (line 88), 'appropriate' (line 164) — ~5 vague instances
Command takes required $1 but no guard for empty argument; bash inline on line 21 would expose directory listing errors
'comprehensive' (line 7), 'relevant' (lines 35, 40), 'appropriate' (line 95) — ~5 vague instances
!bash -c 'ls -la .kiro/specs/$1/ 2>/dev/null || echo ...' embeds user argument in bash -c without sanitization
curl fetches GitHub API to populate an ipset allowlist; compromised or manipulated API response could influence firewall rules; CIDR validation on lines 47-49 provides partial mitigation
'focused updates' (line 5), 'rapid feedback' (line 10), 'signal-rich' (line 30) — ~3 mild vague instances

CLAUDE.md lists npm run commands as the development interface but package.json scripts internally delegate to bun; bun is not listed as a prerequisite in CLAUDE.md, causing failures on systems without bun installed
execFileSync passes { env: process.env } forwarding the complete calling-shell environment including any API keys or credentials to the platform-specific native binary
devDependencies use ^ caret ranges allowing minor/patch version drift on fresh installs; could introduce breaking changes or compromised versions between lockfile updates

rm -rf "$PARENT_DIR" where PARENT_DIR derives from user-supplied $1; empty-string guard prevents empty input but value is otherwise unrestricted, allowing deletion of arbitrary directories (e.g. $HOME, /tmp)

curl fetches GitHub API to populate an ipset allowlist; compromised or manipulated API response could influence firewall rules; CIDR validation on lines 47-49 provides partial mitigation

CLAUDE.md lists npm run commands as the development interface but package.json scripts internally delegate to bun; bun is not listed as a prerequisite in CLAUDE.md, causing failures on systems without bun installed

Three commands (spec-requirements.md:21, spec-status.md:13, validate-gap.md:21) share the identical unsanitized !bash -c '$1' pattern; fix must be applied uniformly across all three to avoid partial remediation
